_contextSnippet: Tumors from mice with activated Met transgene expression demonstrated a high rate of cellular proliferation (Ki67) and greater amounts of active phospho-MET (pMET) by immunohistochemistry. As expected, capmatinib resulted in complete inhibition of MET activation in NF1-MET tumors after 2 days of treatment
details: Successfully showed greater amounts of active phospho-MET in tumors from mice with activated Met transgene expression and complete inhibition of MET activation after 2 days of capmatinib treatment
